mandate: Per-partner analytics: conversion funnels, retention metrics, message volume, revenue attribution, performance badges, admin leaderboard
keyFindings: Revenue attribution uses hardcoded plan values (Starter=$0, Essential=$49, Concierge=$79) — not actual Stripe data
Performance badge thresholds: Green (conversion>70%, retention>80%), Yellow (middle), Red (conversion<40% or retention<50%)
Conversion funnel visualization uses simple CSS bars, no charting library needed
Admin partner table extended with Health column showing PartnerPerformanceBadge per partner
whatWorked: Cross-referencing partner_referrals with couple_metrics for real engagement data. Parallel data fetching in page.tsx for performance metrics and revenue attribution. Simple Tailwind visual indicators instead of charting libraries.
whatFailed: Nothing failed. Pre-existing TS errors from partner-auth's initial placement at /partner/join were resolved by that agent.
recommendationsForNextAgent: When Stripe Connect is added, replace the hardcoded plan values with actual subscription data. The leaderboard query could become expensive at scale — consider materialized views or periodic computation.
spiritContribution: Turned raw referral counts into actionable intelligence for both partners and the founder. The conversion funnel and revenue attribution make the B2B2C channel measurable.
